zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Eleanor Sinclair of Caithness - Countess of Atholl於1457年4月19日出生在蘇格蘭費伏吉卡爾迪時，她的父親William Sinclair 1st Earl of Caithness47歲，母親Lady Marjory Sutherland33歲。她於1475年4月19日與Sir John Stewart 1st Earl of Atholl在蘇格蘭培爾登可爾德登可爾德 凱德拉爾結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和9個女兒的父母。她去世於1518年3月21日在英國蘇格蘭培爾登可爾德享年60歲，埋葬在蘇格蘭培爾登可爾德。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lady Eleanor Sinclair of Caithness - Countess of Atholl于1457年4月19日出生在苏格兰費伏吉卡爾迪时，她的父亲William Sinclair 1st Earl of Caithness47岁，母亲Lady Marjory Sutherland33岁。她于1475年4月19日与Sir John Stewart 1st Earl of Atholl在苏格兰培爾登可爾德登可爾德 凱德拉爾结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和9个女儿的父母。她去世于1518年3月21日在英国苏格兰培爾登可爾德享年60岁，埋葬在苏格兰培爾登可爾德。

en.Balveny Castle, Fife, Fifeshire, Scotlan: Bow of Fife, Fife, Scotland, United Kingdom

nameMeanings.name: Sinclair
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish and English: of Norman origin, a habitational name from Saint-Clair-sur-Elle (Manche), so called from the dedication of its church to Saint Clarus (see   Clare   3).
Americanized form of some similar (like-sounding) Jewish surname.

History: The Scottish family Sinclair (see 1 above) came to southern Scotland in the twelfth century from Huntingdonshire with the de Morvilles. Descendants of these Sinclairs became earls of Caithness in the 15th century. The frequency of the surname in Caithness and Orkney is largely due to the adoption by tenants of the name of their overlord.

variants.name: Clare
meanings: Irish (Clare, Wicklow, and Wexford): habitational name from Clare in Suffolk (an eastern county of England), the center of a major Norman baronial estate since the Norman Conquest. The English placename probably originated as a Celtic river name meaning ‘bright, gentle, or warm’. The surname is not from the Irish county name Clare, which is either from the surname or from a place called with Gaelic clár ‘plank bridge’. Another Norman surname in the southeast of Ireland was de Clere, from Clères in Seine-Maritime (France), named from the local river.

English and French: from the Middle English, Old French female personal name Cla(i)re (from Latin Clara, from clarus ‘clear, bright, famous’) which achieved some popularity, greater elsewhere in Europe than in England, through the fame of Saint Clare of Assisi (see Chiara ). The English surname seems to have been interchangeable with Clear 1.

English: occupational name from an agent derivative of Middle English cley ‘clay’. The work of medieval clayers involved plastering a framework of interwoven twigs with mud to produce wattle-and-daub work.

variants.name: St. Clair
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Sinclair  . This is also a French Canadian and American French altered form of the same surname.
French (Saint-Clair): habitational name from any of several places, e.g. in Normandy, named Saint-Clair (from the dedication of their churches to Saint Clarus). Compare Saintclair .

Haitian (also Saint-Clair): ornamental name or nickname, perhaps ironic, composed of French saint ‘saint’ or ‘holy’ and clair ‘clear’ or ‘lightness’. Alternatively, perhaps of the same French origin as 2 above. Compare Saintclair .

nameMeanings.name: Eleanor
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French respelling of the Old Provençal name Alienor. This has sometimes been taken as a derivative of Helen , but it is more probably of Germanic derivation (the first element being ali ‘other, foreign’ the second is obscure). The name was introduced to England by Eleanor of Aquitaine ( 1122–1204 ), who came from Aquitaine in south-west France to be the wife of King Henry II . It was also borne by Eleanor of Provence, the wife of Henry III , and Eleanor of Castile, wife of Edward I .

variants.name: Ellenor
meanings: Variant spelling of   Eleanor  , the result of blending with   Ellen .

variants.name: Elenor
meanings: From an Old French respelling of the Old Provençal name Alienor. This has sometimes been taken as a derivative of Helen , but it is more probably of Germanic derivation (the first element being ali ‘other, foreign’ the second is obscure). The name was introduced to England by Eleanor of Aquitaine ( 1122–1204 ), who came from Aquitaine in south-west France to be the wife of King Henry II . It was also borne by Eleanor of Provence, the wife of Henry III , and Eleanor of Castile, wife of Edward I .

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Eleanor Sinclair of Caithness - Countess of Atholl was born on 19 April 1457, in Kirkcaldy, Fife, Scotland, her father, William Sinclair 1st Earl of Caithness, was 47 and her mother, Lady Marjory Sutherland, was 33. She married Sir John Stewart 1st Earl of Atholl on 19 April 1475, in Dunkeld Cathedral, Dunkeld, Perthshire, Scotland. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 9 daughters. She died on 21 March 1518, in Dunkeld, Perthshire, Scotland, United Kingdom, at the age of 60, and was buried in Dunkeld, Perthshire, Scotland.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lady Eleanor Sinclair of Caithness - Countess of Atholl est née le 19 avril 1457, à Kirkcaldy, Fife, Écosse, son père, William Sinclair 1st Earl of Caithness, avait 47 ans et sa mère, Lady Marjory Sutherland, avait 33 ans. Elle a épousé Sir John Stewart 1st Earl of Atholl le 19 avril 1475, à Dunkeld Cathedral, Dunkeld, Perthshire, Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 9 filles. Elle est décédée le 21 mars 1518, à Dunkeld, Perthshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 60 ans, et a été enterrée à Dunkeld, Perthshire, Écosse.

de.Balveny Castle, Fife, Fifeshire, Scotlan: Bow of Fife, Fife, Schottland, United Kingdom
de.lifeSummary: Als Lady Eleanor Sinclair of Caithness - Countess of Atholl am 19. April 1457 in Kirkcaldy, Fife, Scotland geboren wurde, war ihr Vater William Sinclair 1st Earl of Caithness 47 und ihre Mutter Lady Marjory Sutherland 33. Sie hat am 19. April 1475 in Dunkeld Cathedral, Dunkeld, Perthshire, Scotland Sir John Stewart 1st Earl of Atholl geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 9 Töchter. Sie starb am 21. März 1518 im Alter von 60 Jahren in Dunkeld, Perthshire, Schottland, United Kingdom und wurde in Dunkeld, Perthshire, Scotland begraben.

Lei sposato Sir John Stewart 1st Earl of Atholl il 19 aprile 1475, nel Dunkeld Cathedral, Dunkeld, Perthshire, Scotland. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 9 figlie. ja.lifeSummary: Lady Eleanor Sinclair of Caithness - Countess of Athollが1457年4月19日にスコットランドフィフキルクカルディで生まれた時，父William Sinclair 1st Earl of Caithnessは47歳，母Lady Marjory Sutherlandは33歳であった。1475年4月19日にスコットランドペルスシュアイアダンケルドダンケルド カスエドラルでSir John Stewart 1st Earl of Athollと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と9人の娘の親であった。彼女は1518年3月21日にイギリススコットランドペルスシュアイアダンケルドで60歳で死去し，スコットランドペルスシュアイアダンケルドにて埋葬された。

ko.Balveny Castle, Fife, Fifeshire, Scotlan: 영국 스코틀랜드 피프 보 프 피프
ko.lifeSummary: Lady Eleanor Sinclair of Caithness - Countess of Atholl(이)가 1457년 4월 19일 스컷랜드 피프 커크캘디에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William Sinclair 1st Earl of Caithness(은)는 47세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Lady Marjory Sutherland(은)는 33세였다. 그녀는 1475년 4월 19일 스컷랜드 퍼스실르 던켈드 던켈드 캐테드럴에서 Sir John Stewart 1st Earl of Atholl(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 아홉 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1518년 3월 21일 영국 스코틀랜드 퍼스실르 던켈드에서 60세의 나이로 사망했으며 스컷랜드 퍼스실르 던켈드에 묻혔다.

Ela casou-se com Sir John Stewart 1st Earl of Atholl em 19 de abril de 1475, em Dunkeld Cathedral, Dunkeld, Perthshire, Scotland. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 9 filhas. ru.Balveny Castle, Fife, Fifeshire, Scotlan: боу оф фиф, фиф, Шотландия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lady Eleanor Sinclair of Caithness - Countess of Atholl родилась 19 апреля 1457 г. в местности кирккалди, фиф, Шотландия, её отцу по имени William Sinclair 1st Earl of Caithness было 47 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady Marjory Sutherland, 33. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir John Stewart 1st Earl of Atholl 19 апреля 1475 г. в местности дункелд кэтедраль, дункелд, пиртшир, Шотландия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 9 дочерей. Она умерла 21 марта 1518 г. в местности дункелд, пиртшир, Шотландия, Великобритания в возрасте 60 лет и была похоронена в местности дункелд, пиртшир, Шотландия.

Se casó con Sir John Stewart 1st Earl of Atholl el 19 de abril de 1475, en Dunkeld Cathedral, Dunkeld, Perthshire, Scotland. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 9 hijas. Murió el 21 de marzo de 1518, en Dunkeld, Perthshire, Escocia, Reino Unido, a la edad de 60 años, y fue sepultada en Dunkeld, Perthshire, Scotland.
